page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server instance configured with 96 vCPUs and 744 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, the system contains 48 physical cores and supports two threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-threaded operations and integer processing. The server achieves top-10% results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth, reaching 288.74 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 124.06 GB/sec for large-block write operations. For specialized workloads, the CPU supports Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which contribute to top-tier prompt processing speeds during LLM inference. However, single-core CPU performance under stress-ng and text generation speeds for large LLM models remain in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-96 offers high memory density per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of unneeded GPU hardware. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This server is optimized for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server configured with 96 vCPUs and 744 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C x86_64 processor operating at 2.3 GHz across 48 physical cores with dedicated allocation. The hardware lacks local storage and GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. In benchmark evaluations, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, static web serving throughput, and prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models, aided by built-in Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Conversely, single-core stress-ng performance and large-model text generation speeds fall into the average tier. This resource profile is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and CPU-driven data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise database and memory-intensive computing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM providing 7.75 GB of memory per core
Intel Xeon 8581C processor with AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions
Top-tier multi-core CPU, static web serving, and Geekbench benchmark scores
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Optimized for in-memory databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
